perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party s hall protect the Confidentia l Information with at leas t t he same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no case, less than reasonable care.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party s hall protect the Proprietary Data with at leas t t he same degree of care that it uses to protect its own confidential information, but in no case, less than reasonable care.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' introduces ambiguity without a clear definition for 'Proprietary Data'. The term 'Confidential Information' is already defined in Section 1. This change implies that 'Proprietary Data' is distinct from 'Confidential Information,' creating a contradiction. It's now unclear whether the Receiving Party has the same obligations regarding undefined 'Proprietary Data' as they do for 'Confidential Information,' which is defined.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Proprietary Data' creates a contradiction because 'Confidential Information' is explicitly defined in the 'Contradicted Text', while 'Proprietary Data' is not. This misalignment of terminology, as per the provided definition, introduces ambiguity and conflict regarding which data the Receiving Party is obligated to protect under the 'Confidential Information' definition. The original text aligns, the change creates ambiguity, therefore it's a 'YES'.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party will immediately notify th e Casino upon the discovery of any lo ss or unauthorized disclosure of the Confide ntial Information.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party will immediately notify th e Casino upon the discovery of any lo ss or unauthorized disclosure of the Restricted Data.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Restricted Data' introduces ambiguity. Section 1 already defines 'Confidential Information,' but now a new term 'Restricted Data' is used without providing a definition. This creates a contradiction and potential legal issues as it is unclear whether 'Restricted Data' is the same as 'Confidential Information.' If they are intended to be the same, using different terms is confusing; if they are different, the obligations relating to each are unclear, especially concerning immediate notification of loss or unauthorized disclosure. The definition of Confidential information from section 1, doesn't define the meaning of Restricted Data
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Restricted Data' introduces a new, undefined term. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information,' but the changed text uses 'Restricted Data' without a corresponding definition. This directly contradicts the existing definition because it creates uncertainty as to whether they are the same, and what obligations exist for 'Restricted Data'. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Receiving Party shall have no obligations unde r this Agreement with respect to Conf idential Information if it can demonstrate tha t the information: ( a) is publicly av ailable at the time of disclosure; (b) be comes publ icly a vailable after di sclosure through no act of the receiving pa rty or its repr esentatives; (c) is in the receiving party’s possession free of obligation; (d)is deve loped by the receiving party without use of the Confidential Information or through other breach of this Agreement; ( e ) is disclosed to the receiving party by a third party without obligation; (e) is not identifiable a s confidential; o r ( f) is required to be publ icly disclosed pursuant to a properly executed subpoena or other regulatory or court order (“Order”), provided the receiving party ( i ) gives r easonable w ritten not ice to the disclosing pa rty, ( ii) gives the disclosing party a reasonable oppor tunity to respond to the Order, and (iii) limits dis closure to tha t portion of the Confidential Information re quired b y the Order.
perturbation.changed_text: Receiving Party shall have no obligations unde r this Agreement with respect to Sensitive Business Data if it can demonstrate tha t the information: ( a) is publicly av ailable at the time of disclosure; (b) be comes publ icly a vailable after di sclosure through no act of the receiving pa rty or its repr esentatives; (c) is in the receiving party’s possession free of obligation; (d)is deve loped by the receiving party without use of the Sensitive Business Data or through other breach of this Agreement; ( e ) is disclosed to the receiving party by a third party without obligation; (e) is not identifiable a s confidential; o r ( f) is required to be publ icly disclosed pursuant to a properly executed subpoena or other regulatory or court order (“Order”), provided the receiving party ( i ) gives r easonable w ritten not ice to the disclosing pa rty, ( ii) gives the disclosing party a reasonable oppor tunity to respond to the Order, and (iii) limits dis closure to tha t portion of the Sensitive Business Data re quired b y the Order.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Confidential Information' to 'Sensitive Business Data' in this section creates confusion. Previously, Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information,' and now section 5 uses another new term - 'Sensitive Business Data' - without clarifying the connection or difference between the two. The contract now implies that obligations regarding 'Sensitive Business Data' are subject to different conditions than 'Confidential Information.' This internal contradiction creates uncertainty about which information is protected and under what circumstances the Receiving Party is released from their obligations. This is especially problematic within the same paragraph of the text.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The original text refers to 'Confidential Information', which is defined elsewhere in the contract. The changed text replaces 'Confidential Information' with 'Sensitive Business Data' without defining 'Sensitive Business Data', leading to a contradiction. The contradicted text explicitly defines 'Confidential Information'. The change creates uncertainty about whether the obligations regarding 'Sensitive Business Data' are the same as those for 'Confidential Information', which is a clear contradiction and falls under the definition of 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall immediately return all Confidential Information received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Confidential Information. Alternatively, Casino may direct R eceiving Party to destroy such Confidential Information, in which case Receiving Party shall confirm such destruction in writing within twenty (20) days thereafter.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon the request of Casino, Receiving Party shall immediately return all Casino Data received in written or tangible form, including all notes, copies, o r media containing such Casino Data. Alternatively, Casino may direct R eceiving Party to destroy such Casino Data, in which case Receiving Party shall confirm such destruction in writing within twenty (20) days thereafter.
perturbation.explanation: Replacing 'Confidential Information' with 'Casino Data' creates a conflict. The term 'Confidential Information' is well defined within Section 1. Now Section 6 introduces a completely new term 'Casino Data' without a corresponding definition. This creates an internal contradiction by potentially narrowing or broadening the scope of the data that must be returned or destroyed. This makes the agreement more open to interpretation in the courts.
perturbation.contradicted_text: “Confidential Inform ation” means any and all confidential and/or proprietary information disclosed by the Casino t o the Receiving Party, which may in clude without limitation: financial information, business information, m arketing d ata, trade secrets, and other data, materials, products, plans, specifications, reports, manuals, computer software or programs, cont ractual relationships and other similar information delivered in any form or media.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Confidential Information' to 'Casino Data' introduces a new, undefined term. Section 1 defines 'Confidential Information,' but the modified Section 6 uses 'Casino Data' without definition, creating an ambiguity as to what data is covered. This contradicts the original contract by potentially narrowing or broadening the scope of data covered under the confidentiality clause, leading to a direct conflict and uncertainty.
